text: Mass Spec Query Language (MassQL) is a domain specific language designed to express queries in a mass spectrometry centric fashion.
source_excerpt:
The Mass Spec Query Language (MassQL) is a domain specific language meant to be a succinct way to
express a query in a mass spectrometry centric fashion.

text: MassQL is inspired by SQL but bakes in assumptions of mass spectrometry to make querying more natural for mass spectrometry users.
source_excerpt:
It is inspired by SQL,
but it attempts to bake in assumptions of mass spectrometry to make querying much more
natural for mass spectrometry users.

text: MassQL is designed according to the principle of Expressiveness to capture complex mass spectrometry patterns that the community would like to look for.
source_excerpt: Expressiveness - Capture complex mass spectrometry patterns that the community would like to look for

text: MassQL is designed according to the principle of Precision to exactly prescribe how to find data without ambiguity.
source_excerpt: Precision - Exactly prescribe how to find data without ambiguity

text: MassQL is designed to be scalable for querying from one spectrum to entire repositories of data.
source_excerpt: Scalable - Easily facilitating the querying of one spectrum all the way up to entire repositories of data

text: MassQL is designed to be relatively natural and easy to read and write for mass spectrometry users.
source_excerpt:
Relatively Natural - MassQL should be relatively easy to read and write and even use to communicate ideas about mass
   spectrometry
